Work as the Dev Team lead, not as a lone coder. Goal: produce a build-ready implementation brief for one operator-facing module intended for the eventual Trading domain in Mission Control. Use the current backlog brief as the core intent: spec one concrete UI module for the Actionable Alpha dashboard that combines sentiment, technical indicators, and fundamentals into one operator decision view. Deliver exactly one markdown spec in projects/market-intel/notes/ that defines: (1) one named module, (2) the exact operator decision it supports, (3) FE layout and hierarchy, (4) required inputs and data contract, (5) QA acceptance checks and edge cases, and (6) one recommended smallest next build slice. Constraints: no broad redesign, no control-plane or runtime changes, no touching unrelated Mission Control pages, and no fake data claims. This is primarily a planning/spec handoff, not a general implementation sprint.

lanePlanSteps.validationFocus: Route auth boundary, upload validation, response contract, and non-persistent audio handling.

lanePlanSteps.purpose: Wire the existing mic button to a narrow recording state machine and connect it to the real transcription endpoint.

lanePlanSteps.purpose: Verify the end-to-end phase-1 operator flow against the plan acceptance criteria before calling it done.

decision.rationale: The brief is clear and already backed by a concrete implementation plan, so execution can start now with the smallest honest implementation sequence.

decision.completionCriteria: Phase 1 is done when the mic button records and stops reliably, audio is transcribed through the backend route, the transcript lands in the composer for manual editing, and failure states are visible and non-destructive.

changesOrFindings: Added /api/transcribe route with multipart upload, MIME/size validation, and stable JSON responses.

unresolvedIssues: MISSION_CONTROL_TRANSCRIBE_API_KEY is not configured in preview/runtime env yet.

orchestrations.requestedPrompt: Sudo, create an implementation plan for speech-to-text integration in Mission Control. You can do a quick crawl through the Nerve repo, to see how they did it in that UI. It should be in your workspace in  /tmp/openclaw-nerve. You don't have to clone their setup, make your own implementation plan based on these findings towards what would work (and look) best. Note: there is already a microphone-icon next to the composer on the Chat page we can connect it to.
orchestrations.promptSummary: Sudo, create an implementation plan for speech-to-text integration in Mission Control. You can do a quick crawl through the Nerve repo, to see how they did it in that UI. It sho...

decision.rationale: Philippe asked for an implementation plan, not execution, and the repo crawl is enough to give a concrete next step without spawning lanes.
decision.orderRationale: No delegation is needed yet because the brief is clear, the target surface is known, and the useful output right now is a scoped implementation plan grounded in the Nerve reference.
expectedOutputs: A recommended STT architecture for Mission Control
A phased implementation plan tied to real Mission Control files
Nerve findings translated into what to keep, simplify, and avoid
decision.validationIntent: Validate by confirming the mic button can record, transcribe, insert clean text into the composer, and fail gracefully across supported browsers before any wake-word or advanced voice UX is added.
decision.completionCriteria: Done when Philippe has a concrete, scoped plan for Mission Control STT with architecture, file targets, phases, and acceptance criteria.
decision.directAnswer: Recommended plan: build click-to-talk speech-to-text for Mission Control Chat first, with browser recording plus backend transcription as the source of truth, and optionally add browser live preview later. Do not clone Nerve’s full voice stack yet. Quick Nerve findings that matter: - Nerve has a dedicated backend STT route at `server/routes/transcribe.ts` with file-size limits, MIME validation, provider switching, and rate limiting. - Nerve’s frontend voice logic lives in `src/features/voice/useVoiceInput.ts`. It records with `MediaRecorder`, optionally listens with `SpeechRecognition`, and can fall back to `/api/transcribe`. - Nerve marks transcribed user messages with a `[voice]` prefix in `src/features/chat/InputBar.tsx` and `src/features/chat/operations/sendMessage.ts`, mainly so the UI and TTS behavior can react to voice-originated input. - Mission Control already has the exact UI insertion point: the disabled mic button in `projects/mission-control/components/chat/MissionControlChatSurface.tsx` next to the composer. Best-fit architecture for Mission Control: 1. Phase 1 should be push-to-talk only: click mic to start, click again to stop and transcribe. 2. Use `MediaRecorde...
